objective: Refresh upstream performance/cache/stream/stability candidate evidence and make the scan readable for repeat milestone review before any cherry-pick.

details: Added a Markdown renderer for upstream candidate scan JSON.
Generated a fresh fetch-backed upstream scan report at current head.
Generated a readable Markdown review report from the scan JSON.
Did not change inference runtime code, LM Studio state, or benchmark promotion status.

candidate_decision.reason: Fresh scan found no bounded candidate that can be promoted under the retained-workload benchmark, quality-gate, candidate-vs-baseline, and live LM Studio validation requirements.
notes: upstream/main has one right-side commit, 8ae2610, focused on Gemma4 bidirectional visual prefill; it is stability/model-coverage work rather than retained LFM2.5 latency evidence.
upstream/neil/gemma4-tool-context has 18 unmatched commits and introduces tool runtime and Gemma4 reasoning/tool guard surfaces; individual optimization commits depend on that broader surface.
upstream/yagil/mlx-dist-non-batched includes model-thread/distributed changes over an 84-file branch surface; the Qwen VLM prompt-thread commit is not a standalone retained LFM2.5 latency candidate.
upstream/will/lfm-2.5-unified only adds a prompting LFM2.5 caching test; the retained branch already has a non-prompting heavy gate plus benchmark and comparison tooling.
The live LM Studio retained LFM2.5-VL validation gate remains blocked before inference by model-index visibility.

validation.command: python3 -m py_compile scripts/upstream_candidate_report.py tests/test_upstream_candidate_report.py scripts/upstream_candidate_scan.py tests/test_upstream_candidate_scan.py

decision.next_action: Keep scanning upstream with the JSON scanner plus Markdown reporter; only inspect or integrate candidates that are bounded enough to benchmark and still defer promotion until live LM Studio validation is unblocked.
